text: Server-Sent Events (SSE) is a standard enabling servers to push real-time updates to browsers over a single HTTP connection. Unlike WebSocket's bidirectional communication, SSE is unidirectional (server-to-client only) but simpler to implement — using standard HTTP and automatic reconnection.
